guest: Ben Levy, Shaan's business partner — connector and community builder behind Hoop Group and founder dinners

business_ideas.idea: Courtside-style content: interviewing interesting rich people in the seats at NBA games
Auctioning lunches/experiences with notable founders and celebrities, charity-style
Paid hardship experiences: a real Survivor-style ordeal people pay serious money to endure
'Everyone needs their Birkin': collectibles/status objects as self-expression businesses
Curated high-end communities and camps for founders (K Academy-style fantasy experiences)

predictions.text: Premium voluntary-hardship and fantasy-camp experiences will become a serious luxury category

dynamics: Shaan hosts his own business partner and plays editor: Ben fires off half-baked ideas and stories from his text-thread firehose, Shaan filters for what's real and names the frameworks. Warm, inside-joke-heavy, five-years-of-partnership shorthand.
frameworks: Barbell method for relationships: lightweight texts or deep in-person time, kill the middle-zone meetings
TMJ — Too Much Juice: the trait of people who make things happen through sheer energy
Engineered luck: deliberately create serendipity through camps, dinners, and connecting people
learnings: Luck can be manufactured like a metric — put yourself in dense rooms and make introductions constantly
Low-effort consistent relationship touches compound into outsized opportunities over years
Shallow scheduled video calls are the worst of both worlds — go async or go deep in person

banger_quotes.quote: I got a bad case of TMJ, too much juice
You want to spend your time on either end of the barbell

numbers_dropped: K Academy (Coach K's Duke fantasy camp): ~150 attendees at the session Ben attended
notable: An entire episode arguing luck is an engineerable system, told through Ben's fantasy-camp and founder-community escapades.
